MAY.subject: Lagham
MAY.title: Lagham News No. 4 [May 1981]
MAY.description: 1st Lagham Newsletter for May 1981. Issue No. 4. Details Cubs' trip to H.M.S. Belfast and the Tower of London; the results of various Cub Scout sporting events; a Patrol Leaders' visit to a Welsh campsite; Troop Camp near Biggin Hill; Venture Scouts' expedition in Dartmoor; comments on past and upcoming theatrical performances by the Scouts; and the upcoming "Cheese and Wine" evening (including A.G.M.).
MAY.transcription:
Lagham News

No.4May 1981

CUB SECTION

On Saturday 25th March the Pack was well represented at a Cross Country event at Tatsfield and were placed 4th overall with our under 9 years of age team winning their age group. Good individual performances from Richard White, Gary Dring and Neil Roffey in their respective age groups. On the football field we drew 5-5 with the pack from Bletchingley on 5th April and were well and truly defeated by Lingfield Oak Pack in our last league match on 12th April by 18-1. Our scorers in the first game were Gary Dring (2), Anthony Wiseman, Stephen Nixon and Chris Rebbeck; our solitary goal scorer in the Lingfield match was Anthony with a penalty. Congrats etc to both Wayne McClean and Neil Roffey on being selected for the Cubs District Team.
The Pack annual outing was held on 15th April. This year we went to the HMS Belfast and the Tower of London. The weather stayed fine for us, thankfully.
Daniel Walker’s cap decided that it liked the look of the Thames more than Daniels head and was last seen floating the way between the Bank and the Belfast. Apart from this minor mishap we all enjoyed the day out.
Jeremy Upton has left the Pack having moved to Kent - our very best wishes go with him.
Silver Arrows have been awarded to Gary Dring and Stephen Strudwick. Well done both of you and also congrats to Neil Roffey who has inherited the Senior Sixer wiggle. Our four newcomers, Kurt Cormack, Alexander Godden, Nicholas Jones and Karl Wick have been presented with the Cub Scout Badges.
John Axtell
CSL.



VISIT TO THE SCOUT CAMPSITE
IN WALES

On the 1st March a group of P.L.’s plus Nigel King (who was standing in as P.L. for Lions) and Skip, Coxun, Bosun and Akela went to Wales to inspect the campsite for the 1981 Summer camp.
We set off at 6 am in Skip’s and Coxun’s car. We made for the M4 which we remained on for almost all the way. We stopped once at Membury for a rest and a ‘Leek’ (St. David’s Day) then our next stop was the campsite.
When we arrived we met the owner Mr. Williams who directed us to the campsite which was about a mile away from his home. The campsite was along a narrow winding road.
When we looked around the campsite it was raining of course (typical Welsh weather) and Wellies were the order of the day. The campsite was excellent in most ways, it was very flat, well drained, it had a stream running along one side and a canal across the top, suitable for canoeing.
The only snag was that the water tap was about ¼ mile away and firewood was rather scarce. The field was perfect for all sports because of its flatness and its short mossy grass.
After seeing the campsite and being most impressed we went to see Llangorse Lake. After that we went up a narrow winding mountain road for lunch. We found a grassy clearing to eat lunch and after lunch, Coxun had some difficulties getting off the slippery grass.
Eventually he got off and we made our way down into the valley. We rejoined the motorway at about 2pm and at about 4pm we stopped at Membury for a bite to eat and a P.L.’s discussion about various confidential matters. We arrived home at about 7.30pm after and enjoyable St. David’s Day in Wales.
Richard Goodell
Scout.



RETURN VISIT TO THE 38th CROYDON
TROOP CAMP NEAR BIGGIN HILL

One weekend about two months ago 9 lads from the troop went on a camp (in huts luckily), with two other troops, the 38th and 48th Croydons. The Campsite was at Downe, near Biggin Hill. Everyone was put in patrols which were called, Super Eagles, Specials, Dirty (Dingle) Dans and Yorkies
The weekend was enjoyable apart from three things:-
1. The weather was awful as it rained all weekend.
2. The providore was only open for 20 minutes all weekend.
3. A leader called Yo (we nicknamed him Grumpy), who confiscated items off unwary campers eg myself (cards), Gavin Chippinton (Radio).
The activities included compass work, Decoding messages, Comando raids, First aid water line and a krazy football competition in 3 feet of mud; there was a golf course at the foot of the hill and a few golf balls were found. At the end of the camp the positions (with members) of the patrols were:-
1st - Specials - James Fox, Edgar Forman, George Padelopoulos.
2nd - Yorkies - Gavin Chippington, Simon Weaverfalck
3rd - Super Eagles - Richard Goodall, Graham Jakes.
4th - Dirty Dans - Warren Jakes, Darrell Law.
in the end we came to the conclusion that the best part of the camp was going home.
Warren Jakes.
Scout.



FROM THE UNIT

Most of our programme during April was taken up with training for the Dartmoor Expeditions, sessions included what equipment is necessary and how to use it, dangers of walking in open country and on Exposure. We had 17 Venture Scouts walking and 3 leading as invigilators. Two of the Unit were completing Queens Scout Award Expeditions and the remainder Venture Scout Awards Expeditions. This all took part over the Easter holiday weekend and turned out to be very successful - as did the weather
We also took up an invite to cater at a motor cycle meeting in East Grinstead which turned out to be very enlightening - we had a visit by a Public Health Inspector! We didn’t exactly make a profit but had a laugh or two and became wiser for any future event.
Next month will be our first weekend sailing on Spirit of Discovery - more about it next time.
Ray Fox
Venture Scout Leader



HMS BELFAST AND THE TOWER
OF LONDON

On the 15th April 1st Lagham Cubs went to see HMS Belfast in London. As we walked down the street from London Bridge Station towards the Belfast, we saw three hot air balloons in the shape of airships. We had to wait a quarter of an hour so we sat on a wall by the River Thames and had something to eat. During this time Daniel Walker’s cap found its way into the River followed by somebody’s apple.
Once on the Belfast we went to a cabin and saw a film about the ship. After the film Wayne McClean tried on an helmet and gloves which gunners used to wear. The guide showed us two shells and told us that the ship has six nine inch guns and nine six inch guns. When the guide had finished Akela said we could wander around the ship. I bought an ice cream and looked over the bridge, Captains cabin and then the ships canteen where you could buy a cake for 2d. We also saw the gun room, Doctor’s quarters and the ship cells.
Left the Belfast over the river by ferry to see the Tower of London. The first thing we noticed were The Beefeaters and the many cannons laying about. We visited the White Tower and saw over the one hundred pieces of armour with lots of guns and swords. As we came out the guards were changing. Next we saw the Crown Jewels which are protected by six inch steel doors. One of the Gold Orbs was missing, but we were told that the Queen was using it.
After a visit to the souvenir shop we caught the underground to Victoria and then by train home via Redhill.
Anthony Wiseman
Cub.



DATES

1. Our Annual Group Gang Shows have been enjoyed both by audiences and cast. Those cubs, scouts and venture scouts who have enjoyed performing at our Shows, now have an opportunity to appear in next years District Pantomime. Those interested should contact Skip or Mr. Alan Oabourne (Smallfield 3489).
2. Congratulations to patrol leader Andrew Duff on winning his age group section of the County Scouts Cyclocross at High Ashurst, Box Hill. He even managed to get his name published in ‘Surrey Scout’ - (see noticeboard!) This year was the inaugural event but in view of its success it has already been decided to hold a county cyclocross in 1982.


CHEESE AND WINE
12th May

Don’t forget out Wine and Cheese evening at HQ on Tuesday 12th May. It would be nice to see all parents and friends coming along. Patrol leaders and venture scouts also welcome.
The evening starts at 7.30pm and our new extension will be open and on show. During the evening we shall devote about half an hour to our Annual General Meeting.
Tickets are not required - just turn up and enjoy yourselves. Please support this social evening - we would love to see you and also all friends of the group. Children are welcome with parents.
DON’T FORGET!!
Skip.


If you know of anyone who has a mini bus they would kindly lend us please contact Skip. This will be used for Scout Camp and will be greatly needed.


Next edition of Newsletter will be 24th May when the editors would like entries to be handed in.

10.subject: Lagham
10.title: Minutes of 1st Lagham (Godstone) Scout Group Executive Meeting 10th June 1986
10.description: Minutes of a meeting of the 1st Lagham Executive Committee held on 10th June 1986.
10.transcription:
[From note stapled to front of document:]

Would be most grateful if you could let me have a written copy of your report.


[From document:]

1st LAGHAM (GODSTONE) SCOUT GROUP EXECUTIVE MEETING
*************************************************


Minutes of the Executive meeting held at the Headquarters on Monday 10 June 1986 at 2015 hours.

PRESENT

Mrs C Cryer        Mr K Hugget        Mr J Dearmer
Mr D Knight        Mr R Fox           Mrs C Carey
Mr D Brown         Mr J Axtell        Mrs F Fox
Mr J Bowers        Mrs J Prescott

APOLOGIES

        Apologies were received from Mr P Bourne, Mrs C Carey and Mrs P Hubery.

The meeting was held by Mr J Dearmer who deputised for the chairman in his absence.

MATTERS ARISING

1 A post meeting note was added to the minutes of the last meeting, to inform the committee of a change in the hall letting fee previously advised to Mrs J Ovenden.

2 The gutter and facia board have not been replaced due to the presence of a nesting bird.
                                                (Mr K Huggett)

3 The heater will be fitted before the winter, Mr R Fox will contact Mr T Hamer about piping for the heater and Mr G Bush will liase with Mr R Fox about the electrics.
                                                (Mr K Huggett)

The secretary read a letter that was received regarding a grant for the heater, Mr J Bowers will contact Mr P Lancaster with reference to the position of the Scout Association to such aid.
                                                (Mr J Bowers)

4 Mr J Axtell has still to contact Mr P Flemming. Mr J Bowers has put out feelers to Orme House School regarding the two buses they have for auction. Mr J Bowers also to contact Mr A Lee.
                                                (Mr J Bowers)

Mr B Gelliot has given us to understand that it is alright to advertise on the bus.

5 It has been requested that the minutes be presented with an action column.
                                                (Mrs F Fox)

6 Mr J Bowers has written to the D.C. who has approved the requests for Thanks Badges, however it was felt that an omission had been made therefore Mrs M Browns' name has also been proposed and approved this will be presented to her at the Group Camp in August.

7 No change has occurred with regard the petrol account.

8 No arrangements have been made with regard the cleaning account.
                                                (Mr B Twigg)


REPORTS

TREASURER'S REPORT

Monies held by Group:
Deposit Account 400.00
Current Account 1587.58
Petty Cash 117.68
2105.26


This much healthier state of affairs is due to the very successful Golden Gala Day, which netted approximately £1300 and also the jumble sale which made £224. Major expenses paid include a new 160lb tent at £162. Thanks must be expressed to Mrs J Prescott for running the events and her helpers, and also to Mrs J Dearmer who helped with the big count up.

BEAVER'S REPORT

The colony now number 18, but numbers will reduce in September as 2 will leave. The Beavers have enjoyed many activities including swimming, a barbeque and sports evening. They also won the cannon racing at the Golden Gala Day. Future activities include a day at the Group camp, a District Beaver picnic at Knowle Park. Mrs P Hubery has recently been appointed as Assistant Beaver Leader.

CUB SCOUT LEADER'S REPORT.

Since the last meeting the pack has increased in numbers to 22 cubs. The pack has taken part in the District Cub Sports and Canoe racing and have also manned the skittles. Activities have also included an evening hike to Crowhurst Church, swimming at the school, tracking and firelighting. Many of the cubs have gained proficiency badges, these have included 7 cyclists, 5 athletes (stage 1), 1 athlete (stage 2), and 2 athletes (stage 3), 7 swimmer (stage 1), and lastly 2 hobbies badges. Future events include the Cub and Group Camp at Blacklands, there will also be meetings on August 11th and 18th, but meetings will resume in earnest on September 1st. Not to be missed is a show called Rainbow Magic '86 at Notre Dame Convent on 19th and 20th September at 7.30 pm. This is being organised by the District Cubs and stars some of our members. Tickets are £2.00 for adults and £1.00 for children, and can be ordered through Mr J Axtell.

SCOUT LEADER'S REPORT.

The Troop have had an excellent time since the last meeting and have been re-organised into 4 patrols with new patrol leader's. Both discipline and helpfulness of the troop is superb during and out of meetings. Several badges have been presented and currently we are running a mechanics course. Recently at the Golden Gala day we were pleased to win the Chariot racing.

VENTURE SCOUT LEADER'S REPORT.

The Unit has now started to get back to normal with the end of exams. The programme has been busy starting with a weekend sail aboard the yacht Penkin. The unit gained second place in the District Cooking Competition held at Brakey Hill recently. The last couple of months unit meetings have included a ten-pin bowling evening, and Volleyball competition. Many of the Unit completed a windsurfing course at Hove this last weekend, most people were able to sail at the end, though only for about 20 feet. Confirmation of Ian Lea's Queen Scout Award has still not arrived. Neil Roffey completed a week aboard the Sail Training Yacht Donald Searle on her delivery to the Dutch coast to start the Tall Ships Race. The unit enjoyed the Golden Gala Day and wish to extend their thanks to the Fund Raising committee for a successful day. The Tinkers were thanked for their expression of thanks to the unit for helping to clear up after the Golden Gala Day.

GROUP SCOUT LEADER'S REPORT.

It was sadly reported that Kay Poulton has decided to take a break as ASL, but it is hoped that she will return later. She has given good service to the section and the Troop made a small presentation to her. Pauline Hubery has been appointed as ABSL and Rex Holman is to be appointed instructor with the pack. The Leader's would like to acknowledge the tremendous efforts and success of Jennifer Prescott and her Fund Raising committee particularly with regards to the Golden Gala Day which was both profitable and enjoyable. The recent coverage of the Group in the Surrey Mirror has been good and they wish to run a story in their yesteryear section. Margaret Errol has completed the composition of the Group Anniversary song, Lagham Scout March. Mr J Axtell attended the Cub Leader's camp at Gilwell and reported that it was the most enjoyable event that he has attended. The Group History has been completed by Mr G Bush, it is to be copied by Mr R Fox and sent to various leaders who will append their own particular histories.

QUARTERMASTER'S REPORT.

The 160lb tent has been purchased, paving stones have been set into the grass at the back on which to light fires. The Tinkers also prepared and loaded the equipment for the Golden Gala Day and finally cleared up. The remaining pram have been cut up saving the handles and wheels for use on the Chariots. The facia board has been replaced, the gutters and drainpipes have still to be done.

FUND RAISING COMMITTEE'S REPORT.

The Golden Gala Day was a great success and thanks must be expressed to all those who helped. Future events include a Jumble Sale On 27th September at 2.30 and a Barn Dance on 25th October at 7.30.

ANY OTHER BUSINESS.

MARQUEE.

A maul is required for the marquee Mr R Fox will investigate the cost

MINI-BUS.

If a new bus is to be bought it will need to be put away after use.

It is with regret that the secretary had to report that Mrs C Carey has resigned her position on the Executive.


Signed ....................................

Dated .....................................

APR.subject: District
APR.condition: Water damaged; hole-punched; fragile paper; torn section of paper missing
APR.title: East Surrey District Newsletter - April 1977
APR.description: East Surrey District Newsletter for April 1977.
APR.transcription:
EAST SURREY DISTRICT
* * * * * * * * * *
N.E.W.S.L.E.T.T.E.R - APRIL 1977
* * * * * * * * * *


It is with regret that I have to announce the resignation of Barry Hillier as A.D.C. (Scouts). Owing to the pressure of business and other commitments, Barry felt that he would be unable to devote the time necessary for the continued development of the Scout Section. On behalf of the District I would like to thank him for all the work which he has put into the Scout Section during the 5 years that he has been A.D.C. (Scouts). I am pleased that he is not severing his connection completely with the Movement as he intends to be involved as and when time permits with Scout Fellowship activities.

    I am pleased to announce that the County Commissioner has endorsed my nomination of John Grossmith as A.D.C. (Scouts). I am sure that we all wish him every success in his new appointment, and I hope that he will be able to count on the support of every Troop Scouter.

DISTRICT ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING this will be held on Saturday 14th May at St. Stephen's School, Hunters Close, South Godstone. The Exhibition will be open from 7.30 p.m. the meeting will be at 8.0 p.m. Formal agendas will be circulated at the end of April. Sectional A.D.C.s will be organising the Exhibits from their sections and these will be set up between 8.0 and 9.0 p.m. on Friday 13th May. I look forward to a 100% turn out for this important District event when we shall have the opportunity of hearing from Mr. Ted Hayden the Association's Executive Commissioner (Administration) and Secretary who will talk about Adult Support in Scouting.

NOMINATIONS for the 5 elected places on the District Executive Committee should be with the District Secretary, Tony Mills, 95, Home Park, Hurst Green. Oxted 4218 by 15th April please.

ST. GEORGES DAY PARADE will take place at Lingfield Parish Church on Sunday, May 1st at 3.0 p.m. A separate notice giving details of arrangements is enclosed for all G.S.L.s and Section Scouters.

COUNTY FIRST AID COMPETITION - congratulations to Dormansland Troop who, having won the District Competition, represented the District at the recent County Competition on finishing 4th. This was a very creditable performance.

JUBILEE EVENTS - reports are now beginning to filter through of several Group's involvement in local Queen's Silver Jubilee Celebrations. As a special effort, I wonder whether Groups with their own Headquarters could give these a Spring Clean - perhaps a coat of paint outside and tidy the surrounding area as a contribution to a better relationship with the public in this Jubilee Year.

SMARTNESS OF UNIFORM - is very important and on occasions leaves much to be desired. In particular, badges need attention. The County Badge is always to be worn to the front of the District Badge at the top of the right sleeve. Have a look next time at Inspection, you might get a surprise.

CUB SCOUT HANDCRAFT COMPETITION - this was a fantastic display 14 out of the 16 Packs in the District entered, and the judges had a very hard time in judging the entries. Congratulations to Godstone Pack on winning this event, to Hedgecourt Maple 2nd and 1st Hurst Green (Manor) Pack 3rd.

DISTRICT CUB SCOUT COMPETITION - eleven Packs entered this keenly fought competition on Saturday, 19th March, and again the judges had a very tiring task in taking the teams through the events. Congratulations to the winners 1st Hurst Green (Manor) Pack with 643 points, 2nd were Blindley Heath with 581 points and 3rd were Oxted Acorn Pack with 580 points.

AIR EXPERIENCE DAYS Every Scout Leader and Venture Scout Leader should have received a notice concerning Air Experience Days during the coming year. If they have not received one will they please let me know.

GROUP A.G.M.s just to remind you that John Grossmith and John Mankelow, who both visited The U.S.A. last year with Ewell Service Unit, are prepared, given due notice, to give a talk on Scouting in the U.S.A. at your Group's A.G.M. Would all G.S.L.s please let me know the date of their Group's A.G.M. as soon as it is known.

    So far I have the following:-

Scout Fellowship A.G.M.         21st April
1st Lingfield                   27th April
1st Oxted                       5th May
1st Bletchingley                ? 6th May
1st Hurst Green                 7th May
Dormansland                     10th May
1st Blindley Heath              17th May
Lagham                          25th June

GROUP EVENTS Dormansland are holding a 'Pub Night' in their H.Q. on 23rd April. Tickets 50p each, including refreshments, obtainable from Group Chairman, Peter Martin. Lingfield 832797, or from any Scouter of the Group.

DATES FOR YOUR DIARY
    Mon. 4th April          Appointments Sub Committee. Mrs. Coutts
    Tues. 26th April        District Team. 15, Hurstlands
    Fri. 29th April         Rehearsal for St. Georges Day Service.
                            Lingfield Parish Church
    Sat. 30th April         1st Hurst Green Fete on the Green, p.m.
    Sun. 1st May            St. Georges Day Service, Lingfield.
    Sat/Sun. 7/8 May        District Camping Competition - Golards Farm
                                                           New Chapel.
    Sat 14th May            Godstone Go-Carts - Afternoon
                            District A.G.M. - evening.
    Fri. 24th May           County A.G.M. Woking
    Fri/Sun 24/26 June      District Cub Scout Camp. Blacklands Farm.

    I am sure we would all like to wish Suzanne Swinchatt and John Grossmith every success and happiness on their marriage.

JUN.subject: District
JUN.condition: Water damaged; hole-punched; fragile paper; damage to paper
JUN.title: East Surrey District Newsletter - June 1977
JUN.description: East Surrey District Newsletter for June 1977.
JUN.transcription:
Group Scouter on County Scout Council - Mr. J. Bowers

        Our thanks to the retiring members of the District Executive Committee for their help and interest during the past year.

DISTRICT EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE will meet on Tuesday, 28th June at 8.0 p.m. at Crowhurst Village Hall.

DISTRICT CUB CAMP will be held at Blacklands Farm, East Grinstead over the week end 24th to 26th June. I hope that all G.S.L.s/Scouters in Charge will make the effort to visit their Packs in Camp, as every Pack in the District will be in camp for the week end.

SWIMMING GALA Please note that the Gala will be held at West Wickham Baths on Saturday, 24th September from 19.00 to 22.00hrs. Details later. Admission by programme only, price 10p. Teams will be charged 50p each team entrance fee.

CHANGE OF DATE The District Cross Country will now be held on Saturday, 8th October and not 24th September as originally stated.

JUBILEE ACTIVITY DAY (Cub Scouts) There will be a meeting of leaders of the various activities at Perry Wood on June 15th at 7.45 p.m.

PLEASE NOTE that the Swimming Gala and the Cross Country Event will be open to all Scout Troops and Venture Scout Units as well as Cub Scouts. I hope that there will be a good representation from all sections.

CANOE INSPECTIONS Trevor Monk - District Water Adviser (Canoeing) has started the annual inspection of canoes. If your Troop or Unit has any canoe which is going to be used in Scouting activities it must have been inspected and issued with a certificate of sea-worthiness before it may be used. If you have canoes for inspection please contact Trevor Monk as soon as possible. His telephone number is Lingfield 833087.

CANOEING AND SAILING COURSES The following courses will be held at Thames Young Mariners Base, Ham, Richmond. Time: 19.00 hrs. Fridays to 17.00 hrs Sundays.

Canoeing - 2/4 September, 16/18 September, 30 Sept/2 Oct. Those who attend will work towards the BCU Elementary & Proficiency Awards.
Sailing - 9/11 September, 23/25 September, 6/8 October. Working for RYA/NSAA Elementary, Intermediate & Advanced Certificates.
For both of these courses all three weekends must be attended.
Fees £15 per course.
Applications forms may be obtained from The Warden, Thames Young Mariners Base, Ham, Richmond. Telephone 01.940.5550.


CARAVANS If a Scouter intends taking a caravan, dormobile or any other vehicle adapted for sleeping purposes to any camp site (other than a permanent Scout or Guide site) they must apply to Headquarters for a Certificate exempting the owner of the site from the necessity to apply for a licence under the Caravan Sites and Control of Development Act 1960. Early application should be made to the Legal Adviser at Baden Powell House giving the name of the owner of the site and his address, together with the address of the site if it is different from that of the owner.

GROUP A.G.M.s - those that I have managed to attend this year have been well organised and it was apparent that much thought had gone into their organisation. The last one of the season is Lagham's on Saturday, 2nd July.

DISTRICT TEAM MEETING - Monday 27th June, 8.0 p.m. 4, Broadbridge Cottages, Broadbridge Lane, Smallfield, Horley.

SCOUTERS MEETINGS (Scouts) - July 6th. 8.0 p.m. Dormansland H. Q.
Firm confirmation for the Swimming Gala to be given at this meeting.
Oct. 5th. 8.0 p.m. In a Public House - venue to be advised.

CLAN MEETINGS - Last week in June - venue to be advised to Clan reps.
          Wed. Sept. 14th - venue to be advised.
          Thurs. Nov. 17th - venue to be advised.

CASH AND CARRY John Mankelow of the Scout Fellowship holds a cash and carry discount card for the warehouse on the Purley Way Trading Estate, Croydon. This is useful to stock up ones camp e.g. baked beans, tinned peas, beefburgers etc. For the card and letter of introduction apply Alan Osborn ADC Scout Fellowship (Smallfield 3489) or John Mankelow Fellowship Treasurer (Oxted 3173)

2

DEC.subject: District
DEC.title: East Surrey District Newsletter - December 1977
DEC.description: East Surrey District Newsletter for December 1977.
DEC.transcription:
EAST SURREY DISTRICT
* * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * *
NEWSLETTER - DECEMBER 1977
* * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * *


QUEEN'S SCOUT AWARD. Congratulations to Neil Burchett of the de Lucy Venture Scout Unit on gaining the Queen's Scout Award. This is the first such Award to be gained for some considerable time by anyone in the District. It was a great pleasure to me to be able to present him with the Badge of the Award at a packed Group Open Evening on 18th November.

MEMBERSHIP SUBSCRIPTION 1978 You will all, no doubt, have read by now in the November Edition of 'Scouting' that the Headquarters Membership Subscription will be increased by 25p. to £1.50 per head for the next financial year. At first sight, this appears to be a tremendous increase, but when the facts are examined, 17p. of the increase goes straight away in increased Insurance Premium per member, and over this figure, Headquarters has no control. The County Membership Subscription has been held to 30p. per head. The District Membership Subscription has been held, with careful budgeting, to 55p. per head. This means that on 1st April the amount of £2.35 becomes due and payable in respect of every person appearing on the Group Census Return.

Chief Scout's Award. Would all Troop Scouters please bear in mind that the next County Presentation is being hosted by East Surrey District on 25th February and in view of this I trust that there will be a good number of Award holders from this District to be presented with their certificates by the County Commissioner. Will Troop Scouters please let me know without further delay as soon as they have boys in their Troop who are ready for their Interview with the D.C.

Sinbad the Sailor will be next year's Pantomime. It will be performed on Friday February 10th at 7.30p.m. and Saturday 11th February at 2.30p.m. and 7.30p.m. at Notre Dame Convent School Hall, Lingfield. Tickets are priced at 65p and 45p. 10% discount is given to parties of 12 or more. An additional 5% will be given on all tickets ordered and paid for by 31st December.

A Cub Scout Basic Course will be held on 14th and 15th January at 1st Lagham. Dennis Brown, A.D.C. (L.T.) has application forms for the course and will be contacting all who need to complete this stage in their Training.

1st Bletchingley Scout Troop Owing to the resignation of Ken Hayes and Chris Hayes from the 1st Bletchingley Scout Troop, all enquiries relating to the Group, for the time being, should be directed to 'Timber' Wood, Lingfield 832438, and for the Troop to either 'Timber' Wood or Barry Staig, Oxted 2793.

A New Year Gathering for Cub Scout Leaders will be held on Saturday 21st January 1978 in the Forman Institute, Blindley Heath. Tickets are priced 50p per head and are now obtainable from Mary Osborn. (Bring your own Drink)

District Scout Leaders Meeting. Will be held on 6th December venue and agenda will be advised.

District Team Meeting will be held at 6, Meadowlands, Hurst Green on Tuesday 10th January at 8.00p.m.

        Diana joins me in wishing you all a Happy and Blessed Christmas and a Peaceful and Prosperous New Year.

JAN.subject: District
JAN.title: East Surrey District Newsletter - January 1978
JAN.description: East Surrey District Newsletter for January 1978. Refers to an enclosed list of updated badge prices which is missing.
JAN.transcription:
EAST SURREY DISTRICT
* * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * *
NEWSLETTER - JANUARY 1978.


CONGRATULATIONS to Mrs. Eileen Walter, Cub Scout Leader 1st Lagham on gaining the Wood Badge.

BADGE PRICES It is regretted that certain badges have had to be increased in price with effect from January 1st. 1978. A new badge price list is enclosed for all section leaders.
Please note that badges will be supplied only against a properly completed badge requisition form, it is not fair to ring up Pat Pettigrew and expect her to drop everything attend to your badges, also please note that badges cannot be supplied unless your account is in credit, far too many groups still expect badges to be supplied on account.

OPERATION MEATBALL Night Exercise for Scout Section is being held at Blacklands over the night 7/8th January. Details of this event are now in the hands of Scout Leaders.

DISTRICT FIRST AID COMPETITION will be held on Sunday 29th January at Lingfield Hospital School at 2.0 p.m. will Troops please advise John Grossmith as soon as possible whether or not it is their intention to enter this event.

DISTRICT TEAM MEETING will be held on Tuesday, 10th January at 6, Meadowlands at 8.0 p.m.

DISTRICT C.S.L.S. SOCIAL planned for January 21st, has been cancelled owing to lack of support.

SINBAD THE SAILOR will be performed on Friday, February 10th at 7.30 p.m. and Saturday 11th February at 2.30p.m. and 7.30 p.m. at Notre Dame Convent School Hall, Lingfield. Tickets are priced at 65p and 45p. Please let Alan Osborn have your orders without delay.

CHIEF SCOUT'S AWARD As mentioned in the last Bulletin, the next County Presentation is being held on Saturday, 25th February 1978 at Oxted County School. Will Scouters please get their boys who are ready for interview to contact me as soon as possible, so that they may be presented with their certificate on that occasion.

THE MINIBUS ACT 1977 - ISSUE OF PERMITS will G.S.L. of any Group which has its own transport please deal with the registration of same under this act as soon as possible in accordance with the instructions contained on Page 1. of Headquarters Notices in January 'Scouting'.

SUMMER CAMP 1978 has your P.L.C. decided on the venue for this year if not, it should be done without further delay.

FEB.subject: District
FEB.title: East Surrey District Newsletter - February 1978
FEB.description: East Surrey District Newsletter for February 1978.
FEB.transcription:
EAST SURREY DISTRICT
************
NEWSLETTER - FEBRUARY 1978
****************


CONGRATULATIONS to the following Scouts on gaining the Chief Scout's Award. D. Sugden - 1st Lingfield; K. Marten, N. Perrin, P. Champkins - 1st Dormansland;P. Holly, D. Vale, D. Brown, R. Johnson, S. Hughes, L. Holley - Tatsfield.

        If there are any further Scouts who are ready for their Interview would they please contact the D. C. without delay as the next County Presentation is on Saturday, 25th February at 7.45 p.m at Oxted County School, Bluehouse Lane, Oxted.

        The main arrangements are in the hands of Alan Osborn and the
District Scout Fellowship. Mary Osborn and the Cub Scouters are organising the refreshments. I hope that all Scouters in the District will do all they can to assist the organisers in the smooth running of the evening.

RESIGNATIONS Mick Gray has resigned as Scout Leader at Godstone and Cub Scout Leader at Tatsfield. The District is most grateful to Mick for the many hours he has given over the years to give Scouting and Cubbing to many boys in Godstone and Tatsfield.

        Please amend your District Directory as follows:-

        Godstone Scout Troop please refer all queries to Brian Knight
        the G.S.L.

        Tatsfield Cub Scout Pack insert, C.S.L. Nigel Shaw, 'Primrose Ridge', Greenway, Tatsfield, Nr. Westerham, Kent. (Tatsfield 343)

        Shelagh Murphy has had to resign as C.S.L. of 2nd Hurst Green
(Moor House School) Pack due to studies connected with her work. I am grateful to Douglas Cole and Peter Kaye of the District Scout Fellowship for agreeing to hold the fort for a short time. If there is a Cub Scouter that anyone knows of who would be prepared to work in an Extension Pack would you please tell Mary Osborn.

OPERATION MEATBALL was an event for Scouts and was staged during the night of 7th January at Blackland Farm, East Grinstead. The operation was attended by just short of 70 boys, and was, I think, enjoyed by all who took part. This was the first event of its kind which I have run, and I would like to thank all of the boys who joined in with such good spirit, and the some 40 adults from Troops, Venture Scout Units, Fellowship and Dads who turned out to ensure the success of the evening - John Grossmith.

        My congratulations to John Grossmith and his team, but particularly to John for the planning which contributed to this successful event.

CLIMBING/HILLCRAFT EQUIPMENT SURVEY - Scout Leaders should have received a letter and questionnaire from Roger Smith, the District's Mountaineering Adviser. I hope that you will respond to this, and give him the information requested.

NOVICE CANOE SLALOM will be held on Sunday, 19th February. If your Troop or Unit has not received an entry form would you please contact Trevor Monk as soon as possible.

OPEN COURSES FOR WATER SPORTS - Ravens Ait, Surbiton, Surrey.
        Enclosed, for Troop and Venture Scout Leaders is information
concerning courses which are offered at the I.L.E.A. Conference and Water Sports Centre which are open to anyone who wishes to take advantage.

BROADSTONE WARREN ORIENTEERING RALLY - Sunday, 2nd April, 1978. Publicity for this event is enclosed for all Section Leaders.

GROUP SCOUT LEADERS MEETING will be held on Tuesday, 7th March at 8.15 p.m. in 1st Hurst Green H.Q. I hope that every G.S.L./Scouter in Charge will be present.

DISTRICT EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE will meet on Wednesday, 15th February at 8.00 pm. in the Vicarage, Blindley Heath.

DISTRICT CUB SCOUT LEADERS will meet at Lagham H.Q. on Thursday, 16th Feb. at 8.0 p.m.

DISTRICT CUB SCOUT HANDCRAFT COMPETITION takes place on Saturday, 18th Feb. Exhibition will be open from 2.30 to 4.0 p.m. when the prizes will be presented.

DISTRICT BADGE contrary to rumours which I have heard circulating round the District, the District badge has not been dispensed with. The non-availability of the badge at the present time is entirely due to the fact that the existing design of the badge is now being produced in colour and supplies should be available at the end of March or the beginning of April.

NOV.subject: District
NOV.condition: Water damaged; hole-punched; fragile paper; tears and hole in paper.
NOV.title: East Surrey District Newsletter - November 1978
NOV.description: East Surrey District Newsletter for November 1978.
NOV.transcription:
EAST SURREY DISTRICT
* * * * * * * * * *
NEWSLETTER - NOVEMBER 1978
* * * * * * * * * * * * *


APPOINTMENT OF DISTRICT CUB SCOUT LEADER I am pleased to announce that John Rowse has been appointed District Cub Scout Leader. His primary responsibility will be to assist Mary Osborn in the day to day administration of the Cub Scout Section in the District and to undertake such other duties as may from time to time be determined. His address is 25, Dewlands, Godstone. 842541

WOOD BADGE congratulations to Xenia Johnston, A.C.S.L. 1st Blindley Heath, on gaining the Wood Badge.

DISTRICT COOKING COMPETITION congratulations to 1st Dormansland on finishing 1st, 2nd and 3rd in the recent cooking competition. The standard this year was very high and all competitors are to be congratulated.

DISTRICT SWIMMING GALA the thanks of all Sections are due to the District Scout Fellowship for conducting such a well organised Gala again this year. Congratulations to 1st Lagham Cub Scout Pack on winning the Cub Scout Trophy and to 1st Hedgecourt on gaining the Scout Trophy. At the end of the Gala I was presented with pairs of Swimming Trunks, but only one has a name 'Stuart Beare'. Also I have 2 pairs of Cub size underpants, no names. If any Cub Scout Leader knows to whom they belong, would they please give me a call. If I do not hear by the end of November, then they will be given to a jumble sale.

DIRECTORY AMENDMENTS
Please amend your directories as follows:-
New Appointment S.L. 1st Godstone Derek Leamon. 52, Hickman's Close, Godstone. 842981
Change of address Eileen Walter (1st Lagham) now 32, Brook Close, E. Grinstead.
Change of meeting days - 1st Lingfield (Apollo) Pack now meets Wednesdays 6.30-8.00.
1st Lingfield (Oak) Pack now meets Tuesdays 6.30 8.00.
Oxted Venture Scout Unit now meets Sundays 2.30.

GROUP SHOW 1st Oxted Group are holding a Group Show in the Barn Theatre, Bluehouse Lane, Oxted at 7.30 p.m. on November 30th and December 1st and 2nd. There are reduced rates for Group bookings apply to Mike Moss Oxted 3322.

CLAN MEETING will be held on Wednesday, 8th November in 1st Lingfield H.Q. Would all Scout Leaders please ensure that at least one Patrol Leader from their troop attends. An A.P.L.s training day is being held in 1st Lingfield H.Q. on Saturday 18th Nov. Details will be circulated to all Troops, and I hope that each Troop will be represented.

DISTRICT CUB SCOUT LEADERS will meet on Wednesday 1st November at Blindley Heath H.Q. Would each Pack please make sure that they are present.

DISTRICT CROSS COUNTRY COMPETITION will be held on Saturday 11th November at 2.30 p.m. Open to every Cub Scout Pack and Troop.

DISTRICT DRAMA AFTERNOON will be held on Saturday 25th November at 2.30 p.m. at Lingfield Hospital School. Although this is primarily for the Cub Scout Section, any one is welcome to attend.

A DISTRICT TEAM MEETING for A.D.C.s only will be held on Tuesday 7th November at 15, Hurstlands, Hurst Green at 8.0 p.m.

THE DISTRICT EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE will meet on Wednesday 22nd November at 8.0 p.m. in Blindley Heath Vicarage.

DISTRICT SCOUT LEADERS will meet on Wednesday 6th December at the Albion, Edenbridge at 8.0 p.m.

DISTRICT 1st AID COMPETITION provisional date Sunday 10th December.

SCOUT OUT 1979 will be held for ALL in the Scout Section over the week end 13-15 July at Rushmoor Arena, Aldershot. The cost will be £2.50 per head for all taking part. I hope that the District will be well represented at this exciting County gathering; last time nearly two thirds of the possible attenders in the Scout Section attended, I hope that on this occasion there will be an even larger contingent from East Surrey. John Grossmith is acting as District Liaison with the County and must have firm numbers and money by 1st April next at the latest.

JAN.subject: District
JAN.condition: Water damaged; hole-punched; fragile paper; tears and hole in paper.
JAN.title: East Surrey District Newsletter - January 1979
JAN.description: East Surrey District Newsletter for January 1979.
JAN.transcription:
EAST SURREY DISTRICT
* * * * * * * * * *
NEWSLETTER - JANUARY 1979
* * * * * * * * * *


DISTRICT FIRST AID COMPETITION Congratulations to the team from Dormansland Troop on winning this competition and to 1st Hurst Green on coming second. It was a great pity that only 4 Troops entered for this event. There appears to be a considerable lack of interest, almost amounting to apathy, for District Events in the majority of Troops. It may also be due to lack of communication, for at Clan Meetings, the Patrol Leaders are ignorant of District Events and more often than not when their attention is drawn to the various events on offer, it is the first they have heard of them. Could I ask that we all resolve, during 1979, to pass on information to all in our sections.

CONGRATULATIONS to Julian Stewart Dormansland on gaining the Chief Scout's Award.

BADGE PRICES it is regretted that with effect from 1st January, the prices of some Badges have been increased. A new Badge Price List is enclosed for each Section Leader.

RESIGNATIONS The following resignations have been received with regret;-
Dick Dredge. G.S.L. 1st Tatsfield
Reg Johnston. G.S.L. and Xenia Johnston, A.C.S.L. Blindley Heath.
Kathleen Coltman C.S.L. 2nd Lingfield (Lingfield Hospital School)
Susan Fullilove. C.S.L. 1st Oxted (Oak) Pack.

A Cub Scout Leader is now urgently required at Lingfield Hospital School if the Pack is to continue after Easter. Although efforts will be made by the existing Scouters in the School, they cannot guarantee, nor should they be expected to run the Pack in addition to their other commitments with the Troop. If there is anyone in the District who would welcome a transfer, or you know of anyone who would be prepared to undertake the leadership of this Pack would you please inform either Roger Perritt on East Grinstead 311151, or let the D.C. know as soon as possible.

PIONEERING COURSE. leading to the Pioneering Badge is being organised by John Bowers and Bill Twigg for 1st Lagham Troop at Perrywood Camp Site over the week end 2nd/4th February. They have kindly opened the course to members of any Troop in the District who would like to attend on a first come first served basis. If any Scouts would like to attend, would Scouters please contact either John Bowers Ling. 833201 or Bill Twigg South Godstone 2115 without delay. The probable cost will be £5 to £6.

OPERATION MEATBALL takes place at Broadstone Warren over night 13th February. All Troops have been circulated with details. Have you sent your entries to Carl Stewart?

FIRE PRECAUTIONS at a recent Group Executive Committee meeting which I was attending, The matter of Fire Drill cropped up. Have you had a Fire Drill recently in your section? If not, what about putting it in to the programme one evening? Are the Emergency Doors locked during the meetings?

15th WORLD JAMBOREE in view of the political situation in Iran this has now been postponed. The World Scout Committee has designated 1979 as World Jamboree Year with international World Jamboree Camps to be held in various parts of the world. Representatives from Surrey, which will include David Walter and David Baker from 1st Lagham Troop will be going to Canada for the Manitoba International Jamboree.
As there is a possibility of overseas groups visiting this country during the Summer of 1979, the County has been asked if it could host such a group. On behalf of East Surrey District I have indicated that we would be pleased to host up to six either boys or leaders, and I hope that the Scout Section, in particular, will give support to this. Further details will be published as and when available.

CLAN MEETING will be held at 1st Lingfield H.Q. on Wednesday, 10th January one representative P.L. from each Troop to attend please.

DISTRICT CUB SCOUT LEADERS meet at Hedgecourt H.Q. on Wednesday 17th January.

CUB SCOUT INSTRUCTION DAY Saturday 27th January.

DISTRICT PANTOMIME 'Man in the Moon' Thurs. 8th, Fri. 9th and Sat. 10th February at 7.30 also Sat. 2.30 p.m. at Notre Dame Convent School Hall, Lingfield. Tickets from Alan Osborn Smallfield 3489

DISTRICT EXECUTIVE meets on Wednesday 14th February. Venue to be announced.

SUMMER CAMPS 1979 Has your P.L.C. decided where Summer Camp 1979 is to be held. I hope that all Troops will have a camp in 1979 and that Forms P.C. are completed in good time. Last year there were several last minute panics which I trust will not be repeated.

S.O.S. from Alan Osborn. Tickets for the pantomime are selling very slowly. Why not have a night out take your Troop, Pack or Unit discount for party bookings.

Part of the profits go to the District, and thereby help to keep the Membership Fee lower than it would be.

FEB.subject: District
FEB.condition: Water damaged; hole-punched; fragile paper; tears and hole in paper.
FEB.title: East Surrey District Newsletter - February 1979
FEB.description: East Surrey District Newsletter for February 1979.
FEB.transcription:
EAST SURREY DISTRICT
* * * * * * * * * *
NEWSLETTER - FEBRUARY 1979
* * * * * * * * * * * * *


CONGRATULATIONS to Peter Watson - Dormansland on gaining the Chief Scout's Award to Mike Steer - S.L. 1st Lingfield on gaining the Wood Badge to David Daniels a Scout in 1st Hurst Green Troop who was the winner in the Competition organised by 'Scouting' Magazine for posters depicting accidents anywhere.

OPERATION MEATBALL was much enjoyed by the 59 boys who took part from all but 3 Troops in the District. Our thanks to members of the Fellowship, Scouters and others who helped in the organisation, on a very cold night.

APPOINTMENT OF DISTRICT CHAPLAIN I am pleased to announce that the Reverend Alan Ramsay, A.K.C., The Vicarage, Vicarage Road, Lingfield (Tel.Ling. 832021) has accepted my invitation to join the District Team as District Chaplain. Please contact him direct if you need any assistance on the religious aspect of your activities.

ST.GEORGES DAY PARADE will be held on Sunday, 29th April, 1979 at Lingfield Parish Church at 3.0 p.m. Full details will be circulated to G.S.L.s and Section Leaders nearer the event.

APPLICATIONS FOR LOANS AND GRANT AID Before an application for a loan or Grant Aid is made by either Groups or individuals to the Local Education Authority, Scout Headquarters or any other source, they must be referred to the D.C. for his support and approval. They must never be sent to any Grant Aiding or loan funding body direct. Please see Rule 27 of P.O.R. Part 1.

BROADSTONE WARREN EVENTS enclosed with this Newsletter, for G.S.L.s, is a programme of events at Broadstone Warren. Would they please draw the attention of all in their Groups to this.

DUKE OF EDINBURGH'S AWARD - an Award Presentation will be held on Tuesday, 27 March at 8.0 p.m. at Limpsfield Grange School, Bluehouse Lane, Oxted. when awards will be made by Miss Pearl Clark, the Assistant U.K. Director and S.E. Regional Officer of the Award Scheme. If any leaders have any participants nearing completion of an Award, would they please aim for this date, so that their Awards may be made at this presentation.

CYCLO CROSS Sunday, 18th March. High Ashurst Camp Site, Box Hill. The Competition will be in two sections Junior (Under 14) and Senior (Under 17) on the day of competition. Teams to consist of 3 riders, plus one substitute. Entry fee of £1 per team. Entry forms to be returned by Monday, 5th March to the Area Youth and Community Service, Bridge House, Bridge Street, Leatherhead. Full details will be found in the S.E.Area Youth & Community Teamsheet copy of which goes to your G.S.L. who has full details of this and the following course.

Pre-driver Training Course for Beginners on Sat. 7th April. Goodwood Driving Centre, Goodwood, Nr. Chichester. From 10.15 to 4.45 p.m. for those aged 15 years and over.

DISTRICT PANTOMIME Once again may I remind you about the Pantomime 'Man in the Moon' Thursday 8th, Friday 9th and Saturday 10th February at 7.30 p.m. also Sat. 2.30 p.m. at Notre Dame Convent Hall, Lingfield. Tickets still available from Alan Osborn. (Smallfield 3489).

SCOUT OUT Rushmoor Arena, Aldershot from Friday evening 13th July to Sunday 15th July. I do hope that ALL Scouts in East Surrey will be given the opportunity of attending this County event. This will be an exciting and interesting week end for the boys and something which they should be encouraged to attend.

DISTRICT SCOUT LEADERS MEETING will be held on Wednesday, 14th February at the Albion, Edenbridge at 8.0 p.m.

COUNTY CHIEF SCOUT'S AWARD PRESENTATION will be held on Saturday 17th February in the Reigate area.

DISTRICT CUB HANDCRAFT COMPETITION will be held on Saturday 24th February in 1st Hurst Green's H.Q: Viewing is from 2.30 to 3.30 p.m. If you have never seen the display of work done by the Cubs please make the effort to attend you may be surprised!

FUTURE EVENTS
Wed. 7th March.      Clan Meeting, Lingfield.
Mon 12th March       Meeting for G.S.L.s, Secretarys & Treasurers and Chairmen. Venue to be advised.
Wed.14th March       District C.S.L.s meeting - Godstone H.Q.
Tue, 20th March      District Team meeting 21, Langsmead, B. Heath.
Sat.12th May         District A.G.M. and Barn Dance.


GROUP ANNUAL GENERAL MEETINGS Would G.S.L.s please inform me of the date of their Group's A.G.M. as soon as the date has been fixed, as I like to attend each one, if at all possible.

OCT.description: East Surrey District Newsletter for October 1986. Includes attached autumn programme of Tandridge PHAB Club (Physically Handicapped and Able Bodied), a charity "which exists to further the integration of the physically handicapped into the community."

APR.title: District Executive Minutes April 1986
APR.description: Minutes of the meeting of the District Executive held on 14th April 1986.

MAY17.subject: District
MAY17.condition: Minor damage to top of paper.
MAY17.title: Report on the District St. George's Day Parade
MAY17.description: A report in the County Border News of the East Surrey District St. George's Day Parade in Oxted.
MAY17.transcription:
County Border News    3rd May 2017


East Surrey District Scouts held annual St George’s Day Parade in Oxted with over 300 uniformed Beavers, Cubs, Scouts and Explorers from all over East Surrey, with accompanying uniformed leaders. The parade started at the Tandridge District Council offices, and proceeded up Station Road East into Bluehouse Lane, going up to Oxted School. The St. George’s Day event gave all members, present and past, a chance to reflect on their Scout Promise and their connection to the wider Scout movement.
Photos kevin black

HAR.subject: Village
HAR.title: Photograph of a harvest scene
HAR.description: A photograph of a group of people alongside a horse and cart loaded with hay at harvest. Assumed to be South Godstone. On reverse: "SMITH & Co., CLARENDON STUDIO, REDHILL" and handwritten text "5228". The photo is set correctly within the frame, which is crooked.
